headline_drivers: Fiscal Q2 2026 earnings beat and raise: EPS $0.49 vs $0.47 consensus and revenue $4.91B vs $4.75B (+~15% YoY), with management guiding FY26 toward the high end of prior outlook and emphasizing improving cash flow and ongoing debt reduction (earnings release and coverage, 2026-05-12)

Post‑print analyst re‑rating wave: JPMorgan lifted its target to $55 (from $49, overweight), Truist to $58 (from $50, buy), Stifel to $54 (from $47, buy), and Baird to $58 (from $50, outperform), collectively implying further upside from current levels and reinforcing a bullish narrative (analyst notes summarized on MarketBeat, 2026-05-13)

Momentum / re‑rating after Vestis spin‑off: multiple outlets highlight Aramark’s post‑spin, more focused structure, margin improvement, and leverage trending down, driving a shift from ‘turnaround’ to ‘momentum recovery’ story and pushing the stock to fresh 52‑week highs with rising volumes (Ad-hoc-news overview and related coverage, late April–May 2026)
summary: The sharp move in Aramark is fundamentally driven by a clean fiscal Q2 beat on both EPS ($0.49 vs $0.47) and revenue ($4.91B vs $4.75B), coupled with higher full‑year guidance and management commentary about improving cash flow and ongoing deleveraging. That print triggered a cluster of bullish analyst actions—JPMorgan, Truist, Stifel and Baird all raised price targets into the mid‑$50s range with Buy/Overweight/Outperform ratings—supporting a re‑rating of the post‑Vestis spin‑off story as a higher‑quality, momentum recovery name. The stock is also benefiting from technical follow‑through: new 52‑week highs, a multi‑week uptrend, and volume ~3–4x the 10‑day average, which are amplifying the fundamental catalyst rather than moving the stock on technicals alone.
